html:
[1054]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生平无所愿，愿作乐中筝 - Desire to be a zither in music, symbolizing a wish to be part of something greater or to serve a purpose in harmony.
  • 得近佳人纤手指，呀罗裙上放娇声 - Wish to be close to a beautiful woman's delicate fingers and to hear her playful sounds on a silk skirt.
  • 便死也为荣 - Even death would be honorable if one could fulfill this wish.
  • 展转不寐 - Tossing and turning unable to sleep, indicating restlessness.
  • 早起伺之 - Wake up early and wait for something, suggesting anticipation.
  • 容更鲜妍 - Face that is even more fresh and beautiful.
  • 以金盆洁手，玉腕兰芽，香气芬馥，扑出窗棂 - Scene where a woman cleans her hands in a gold basin, with her white wrists and delicate fingers, and the fragrance of her scent fills the room.
  • 生恐舟人知之，不敢久视 - Man is afraid that the boatman might know about his feelings and is therefore hesitant to look for too long.
  • 乘间以前词书名字，从门隙中投入 - Man takes the opportunity to write down his name and the words of his poem and slips them through a crack in the door.
  • 岂意庾子山复见今日耶 - Who would have thought that Yu Zishan would see such a thing again today? Reference to the poet Yu Zishan, suggesting surprise or nostalgia.
  • 生平耻为贩夫妇，若与此生偕伉俪，愿毕矣 - All my life I have been ashamed to be a merchant's wife, but if I could marry this person, my wish would be fulfilled. Desire for a higher social status.
  • 自是启朱户，露半体，频以目挑 - Woman opens a red door, shows part of her body, and frequently looks at the man with her eyes, indicating flirtation.
  • 畏父在舟，倏启倏闭，终不通一语 - Woman is afraid her father might see her and therefore quickly opens and closes the door, never speaking a word.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 筝 - The zither, a traditional Chinese musical instrument, often used in classical literature to symbolize harmony or artistic expression.
  • 佳人 - Term for a beautiful woman, often used in classical literature to describe an idealized female figure.
  • 罗裙 - Silk skirt, symbolizing elegance and femininity.
  • 庾子山 - Yu Zishan, a famous poet from the Southern Qi and Liang dynasties, known for his lyrical and romantic poetry.
  • 贩夫妇 - Reference to a merchant's wife, often used to describe a lower social status.

Proper Nouns

  • 筝 - The zither.
  • 佳人 - A beautiful woman.
  • 罗裙 - A silk skirt.
  • 庾子山 - Yu Zishan, a famous poet.
  • 贩夫妇 - A merchant's wife.

[1055]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 停午 - Refers to midday or noon, often used to describe a specific time of day.
  • 主人出舟理楫 - Literally means 'the host leaves the boat and tidies the oars,' indicating the start of a journey or departure.
  • 女隔窗招生密语曰 - Literally means 'the woman calls out to the man through the window and whispers secretly,' suggesting a private and intimate moment.
  • 夜无先寝 - Literally means 'do not go to bed first at night,' implying a request to stay awake or delay sleep.
  • 妾有一言 - Literally means 'I have something to say,' a common phrase used by women in classical Chinese to express a personal message.
  • 生喜不自胜 - Literally means 'the man is so happy he can hardly contain himself,' indicating intense joy.
  • 惟恨阳乌不速坠也 - Literally means 'only regretting that the sun does not fall quickly,' expressing a desire for the day to end sooner.
  • 新月微明 - Literally means 'the new moon is faintly bright,' describing a dimly lit night.
  • 轻风徐拂 - Literally means 'a gentle breeze softly brushes,' indicating a mild and pleasant wind.
  • 女开半户 - Literally means 'the woman opens the window halfway,' suggesting a partially open window.
  • 君室中有妇乎 - Literally means 'Is there a wife in your household?' a direct question about marital status.
  • 未也 - Literally means 'no,' a simple negative response.
  • 妾贾人女 - Literally means 'I am a merchant's daughter,' providing information about the woman's background.
  • 小字玉娥 - Literally means 'my given name is Yu'e,' a personal name.
  • 幼喜弄柔翰 - Literally means 'I enjoyed playing with soft writing tools when I was young,' indicating a love for writing.
  • 承示佳词 - Literally means 'I received your excellent poetry,' showing appreciation for the man's writing.
  • 逸思新美 - Literally means 'your thoughts are fresh and beautiful,' praising the man's creativity.
  • 君一片有心人也 - Literally means 'you are a person with a heart,' indicating that the man is sincere and caring.
  • 愿得从伯鸾 - Literally means 'I wish to follow Boyuan,' a reference to a historical figure known for his loyalty and devotion.
  • 齐眉德曜足矣 - Literally means 'to be on equal footing with De Yao is enough,' expressing a desire for a harmonious and respectful relationship.
  • 倘不如愿 - Literally means 'if it is not as wished,' indicating a hypothetical situation.
  • 有相从地下耳 - Literally means 'we will meet in the afterlife,' suggesting a deep commitment even beyond life.
  • 慕君才华 - Literally means 'I admire your talent,' expressing admiration for the man's abilities.
  • 不羞自献 - Literally means 'I am not ashamed to offer myself,' indicating a willingness to be intimate.
  • 君异日富贵 - Literally means 'when you become wealthy and noble in the future,' referring to the man's potential success.
  • 万勿相忘 - Literally means 'do not forget me,' a request for remembrance.
  • 阳侯、河伯实闻此言 - Literally means 'Yang Hou and He Bo actually heard these words,' invoking the gods of the sea and rivers to witness the promise.
  • 无能济河 - Literally means 'unable to cross the river,' suggesting an inability to fulfill the promise.
  • 舟子在前，严父在侧 - Literally means 'the boatman is in front, and my strict father is beside me,' indicating the presence of witnesses.
  • 难以尽言 - Literally means 'it is difficult to say everything,' suggesting that some things cannot be fully expressed.
  • 某月某日 - Literally means 'on a certain day of a certain month,' a specific date.
  • 舟至涪州 - Literally means 'the boat arrives at Fuzhou,' indicating a destination.
  • 父偕舟人往赛水神 - Literally means 'my father and the boatman go to worship the water god,' describing a religious ritual.
  • 日晡方返 - Literally means 'they return at dusk,' indicating the time of return.
  • 君来当为决策 - Literally means 'when you come, we will make a decision,' suggesting a future meeting to finalize plans.
  • 勿以纡道失期 - Literally means 'do not delay the appointment by taking a detour,' advising the man to be punctual.
  • 使妾望眼空穿也 - Literally means 'let me look into the distance in vain,' expressing frustration at waiting.
  • 敬如约 - Literally means 'I will keep the promise,' indicating a commitment to the agreement.
  • 生欲执其手，女谨避不可犯 - Literally means 'the man wants to hold her hand, but the woman carefully avoids it,' showing the woman's resistance to physical intimacy.
  • 其父呼女，女急掩门就寝 - Literally means 'her father calls her, and she quickly closes the door and goes to bed,' indicating the end of the interaction.
  • 生恍惚如在柯蚁梦中 - Literally means 'the man feels dazed as if in a dream,' describing a state of confusion or disorientation.
  • 五夜目不交睫 - Literally means 'five nights without closing one's eyes,' indicating insomnia or restlessness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伯鸾 - Refers to Boyuan, a historical figure known for his loyalty and devotion, often used as a model of fidelity.
  • 德曜 - Refers to De Yao, another historical figure known for his virtue and wisdom, often used as a model of moral excellence.
  • 阳侯、河伯 - Refers to Yang Hou and He Bo, the gods of the sea and rivers, respectively, often invoked in classical Chinese literature to witness important events or promises.
  • 涪州 - A place name, referring to Fuzhou, a city in Sichuan Province, China.
  • 赛水神 - Refers to a ritual of worshiping the water god, a common practice in ancient China to seek blessings or protection from water-related disasters.
  • 日晡 - Refers to dusk or late afternoon, a specific time of day.

[1056]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次日 - Refers to the 'next day,' a common temporal marker in Classical Chinese.
  • 舟泊荆江 - Literally 'boat moored at Jingjiang,' indicating the location where the story takes place.
  • 群从促行 - Means 'the group urged him to go,' where '群从' refers to a group of people and '促行' means to urge or hurry someone to depart.
  • 生徘徊不忍去 - Literally 'the young man hesitated and did not want to leave,' describing the protagonist's reluctance to depart.
  • 促之再三 - Means 'urged him repeatedly,' emphasizing the persistence of the group.
  • 简装登岸 - Literally 'packed lightly and went ashore,' indicating the protagonist's preparation for departure.
  • 伫立顾望 - Means 'stood still and looked back,' describing the protagonist's lingering gaze.
  • 女亦从窗中以目送生 - Literally 'the woman also sent him off with her eyes from the window,' indicating the woman's farewell gesture.
  • 粉黛淫淫，有泪痕矣 - Literally 'her face was wet with tears,' describing the woman's emotional state.
  • 生欷歔哽咽 - Means 'the young man sighed and choked with emotion,' depicting his emotional reaction.
  • 顷之，轻舟持帆，迅速如飞 - Literally 'soon, the light boat held its sail and moved swiftly like flying,' describing the rapid departure of the boat.
  • 生益不胜情 - Means 'the young man felt even more overwhelmed by emotion,' emphasizing his emotional turmoil.
  • 入谒守帅 - Literally 'went in to pay respects to the military governor,' indicating the protagonist's visit to the military commander.
  • 心摇摇如悬旌 - Literally 'his heart was swaying like a flag,' metaphorically describing his emotional instability.
  • 帅屡叩之，不能举词 - Means 'the general repeatedly questioned him, but he could not speak,' indicating the protagonist's inability to respond.
  • 惟辞帅欲往谒故友，数日复来 - Literally 'he only said he wanted to visit an old friend and would return in a few days,' explaining the protagonist's request to the general.
  • 军务倥偬，急需借箸 - Literally 'military affairs are urgent, and I urgently need your help,' indicating the general's explanation for not allowing the protagonist to leave.
  • 命使洁幸舍，治供具，馆生 - Literally 'ordered servants to clean the guesthouse, prepare supplies, and lodge the young man,' describing the general's hospitality.
  • 生逡巡就旅舍，陴守甚严 - Literally 'the young man hesitated and went to the guesthouse, where the guards were very strict,' indicating the protagonist's reluctance and the strict security.
  • 生度不得出，恐失前期 - Literally 'the young man thought he couldn't leave and feared he would miss his appointment,' explaining his anxiety.
  • 逾垣逸走 - Literally 'climbed over the wall and escaped,' describing the protagonist's desperate attempt to leave.
  • 沿途问讯，间关险阻 - Literally 'asked for directions along the way, encountering many difficulties,' indicating the challenges he faced during his escape.
  • 如期抵涪州 - Literally 'arrived at Fuzhou on schedule,' showing that he successfully reached his destination.
  • 客舟云集，见一水崖，绿阴拂岸，女舟孤泊其下 - Literally 'many boats gathered, and he saw a riverbank with green shade brushing the shore, where the woman's boat was moored alone,' describing the scene where he finds the woman.
  • 女独倚篷窗，如有所待 - Literally 'the woman stood alone by the window, as if waiting for something,' indicating her anticipation.
  • 见生至，喜动颜色 - Literally 'when she saw him come, her face lit up with joy,' describing her reaction.
  • 嘱生水急，绁缆登舟 - Literally 'she instructed him to tie up the boat quickly,' giving instructions to the protagonist.
  • 生以手解维欲登，水势汹涌，力不能持 - Literally 'the young man tried to untie the boat and board, but the water was so strong that he couldn't hold on,' describing the dangerous situation.
  • 舟逐水漂漾，瞬息顺流，去若飞电 - Literally 'the boat was swept away by the current, moving swiftly like lightning,' describing the rapid departure of the boat.
  • 生自岸叫呼，女从舟哭泣 - Literally 'the young man shouted from the shore, and the woman cried from the boat,' depicting their emotional reactions.
  • 生沿河渚狂走十余里，望舟若灭若没，不复见矣 - Literally 'the young man ran madly along the riverbank for more than ten li, looking at the boat as if it were disappearing, and he could no longer see it,' describing the protagonist's desperate search.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 荆江 - Refers to the Jing River, a tributary of the Yangtze River in Hubei Province.
  • 守帅 - Refers to the military governor or commander of a region.
  • 涪州 - Refers to Fuzhou, a city in Sichuan Province.
  • 水崖 - Refers to a riverbank or shore.
  • 篷窗 - Refers to a window in a boat, often covered with a canopy or awning.
  • 郎君 - A respectful term for a young man or husband.
  • 信士 - Refers to a man of integrity or faithfulness.

[1057]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 涕泗而回故里 - Return to one's hometown in tears, emphasizing deep sorrow.
  • 气息奄奄 - Gasping for breath, indicating a critical state of health.
  • 寝食俱废 - Neglected both sleep and food, indicating extreme preoccupation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 晚 - Refers to the time of day, significant in the narrative.
  • 或谓 - Reflects the common practice of attributing statements to unnamed sources.
  • 缆断 - Commonly used in maritime contexts to describe accidents at sea.
  • 适琼琼之假母薛媪者 - Indicates the social structure where foster parents play significant roles.
  • 供奉内庭 - Reflects the hierarchical nature of the imperial court.
  • 行抵汉水 - Specifies a geographical location, adding context to the story.
  • 急命长年绁起 - Reflects the urgency and authority of the situation.
  • 覆以纻絮 - Reflects traditional methods of providing warmth and comfort.
  • 调以苏合 - Indicates the use of herbal medicine, reflecting traditional Chinese medical practices.
  • 诘其姓氏 - Reflects the importance of personal identity in social interactions.
  • 字人无也 - Indicates the cultural significance of betrothal and marriage.
  • 出其词为信 - Reflects the use of poetry as a form of commitment in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 素契重生 - Indicates the importance of personal relationships and reputation.
  • 修容不整 - Reflects the cultural emphasis on personal appearance and grooming.
  • 扃户深藏 - Indicates the practice of seclusion, often associated with mourning or withdrawal.
  • 刺绣自给 - Reflects the role of women in traditional crafts and self-sufficiency.
  • 思生之面 - Reflects the emotional depth of the character's feelings.
  • 梦呼生名而不觉也 - Reflects the power of dreams in expressing subconscious desires and emotions.

Proper Nouns

  • 女父 - The father of the girl.
  • 琼琼 - The name of the girl being served in the inner court.
  • 薛媪 - The foster mother of Qiongqiong.
  • 长安 - The capital city, now known as Xi'an.
  • 汉水 - The Han River, a major river in central China.
  • 涪州 - A place name, likely referring to a city in Sichuan Province.
  • 黄生 - The name of the man the girl is betrothed to.

[1058]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一日 - Temporal expression meaning 'one day' or 'at one time.'
  • 胡僧 - Literal meaning 'foreign monk,' referring to a monk from a non-Han Chinese background, often associated with Buddhism.
  • 直抵其室 - Means 'directly to her room,' indicating a direct and unannounced visit.
  • 募化 - A Buddhist term for alms-giving or seeking donations for religious purposes.
  • 弟子堕落火坑 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'a disciple who has fallen into the fire pit,' symbolizing a state of moral or spiritual degradation.
  • 宿缘未了 - Refers to 'unfinished karmic ties' or 'unresolved past connections.'
  • 望师指迷津 - A polite request for guidance, meaning 'I hope the master will point out the way to enlightenment.'
  • 诚念皈依 - Means 'sincerely seeking refuge or enlightenment.'
  • 尘劫 - Refers to 'dust and劫,' a Buddhist term for the cycle of rebirth and suffering.
  • 玉坠 - A jade pendant, often symbolizing purity or spiritual protection.
  • 佩之可解 - Means 'wearing it can resolve the issue.'
  • 勿轻离衣裾 - A cautionary phrase meaning 'do not remove it from your clothing lightly.'
  • 心窃异之 - Means 'secretly found it strange.'
  • 未敢泄于媪也 - Indicates 'did not dare to reveal it to the woman (媪).'
  • 杳然无踪 - Means 'completely disappeared without a trace.'
  • 若醉若狂 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'as if drunk or mad,' describing a state of distraction or obsession.
  • 功名无复置念 - Means 'no longer thinking about success or fame.'
  • 穷途资尽 - Refers to 'being at the end of one's resources,' indicating financial or emotional exhaustion.
  • 每望门投止 - Means 'often seeking shelter at people's doors,' describing a state of homelessness or desperation.
  • 荒林 - Refers to a 'desolate forest.'
  • 古刹 - Means 'an ancient temple.'
  • 趺坐入定 - Describes a monk in meditation, with '趺坐' meaning 'cross-legged sitting' and '入定' meaning 'entering a meditative state.'
  • 五体投地 - A gesture of deep reverence, meaning 'prostrating oneself with five points of the body touching the ground.'
  • 了生死 - Refers to 'understanding the cycle of birth and death,' a key Buddhist concept.
  • 涪州 - A place name, referring to a specific location in ancient China.
  • 天吴漂没 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'lost in the vast ocean,' symbolizing a separation or disappearance.
  • 圣僧也 - Means 'a holy monk.'
  • 叩问 - Refers to 'inquiring humbly.'
  • 大丈夫致身青云 - Means 'a true man achieves high office,' referring to attaining success in one's career.
  • 亢宗显亲 - Refers to 'glorifying one's ancestors and family.'
  • 迷念欲海 - Means 'being lost in the sea of desire,' a metaphor for being trapped in worldly attachments.
  • 非夫矣 - Means 'not a true man,' indicating a failure to live up to one's potential.
  • 迫之再三 - Means 'persistently pressured.'
  • 戒行 - Refers to 'observing precepts or vows.'
  • 应制 - Means 'responding to imperial summons,' referring to taking the imperial examination.
  • 通籍 - Refers to 'becoming an official,' indicating success in the imperial examination.
  • 金部郎 - A title, referring to an official in charge of finances.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 胡僧 - In Chinese history, '胡' often referred to people from Central Asia or other foreign regions, and '胡僧' specifically refers to Buddhist monks from these areas.
  • 募化 - A practice common in Buddhism, where monks seek alms or donations to support their religious activities.
  • 宿缘未了 - A Buddhist concept referring to karmic connections from past lives that have not been resolved.
  • 玉坠 - In Chinese culture, jade is often associated with purity, protection, and spiritual significance.
  • 涪州 - A historical place name, referring to a region in present-day Chongqing, China.
  • 大丈夫致身青云 - A Confucian ideal, emphasizing the importance of achieving success and glory for one's family and ancestors.
  • 迷念欲海 - A Buddhist metaphor for the cycle of desire and suffering.

Proper Nouns

  • 胡僧 - A general term for a foreign monk.
  • 涪州 - A historical place name.
  • 金部郎 - A specific title for an official in charge of finances.

[1059]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 敛怨中外 - Gathering resentment both inside and outside the court
  • 不忍背女初盟 - Could not bear to break his initial promise to the girl
  • 不数石家绿珠矣 - Not inferior to Shi’s Green Pearl, known for her exceptional beauty
  • 布素缟衣 - Wore a plain white robe
  • 云鬓不理 - Hair was not combed
  • 掷之于地 - Threw it on the ground
  • 诸客贺吕得尤物 - Guests congratulated Lü on obtaining a rare beauty
  • 一白马突至厩争枥 - A white horse suddenly rushed to the stable and competed for the manger
  • 啮伤群马 - Bit and injured the other horses
  • 白马从堂奔入内室 - White horse ran from the hall into the inner chamber
  • 则寂无所见 - But there was no sign of it

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吕用之 - Historical figure who held significant political power during the late Tang Dynasty
  • 长安 - Capital city of the Tang Dynasty, known for its cultural and political significance
  • 薛媪 - Old woman, likely a reference to a specific person or a general term for an elderly woman
  • 石家绿珠 - Shi Chong’s concubine Green Pearl, known for her exceptional beauty and tragic story
  • 厩 - Stable or place where horses are kept
  • 枥 - Manger or feeding trough for horses
  • 堂 - Hall or main room in a house
  • 内室 - Inner chamber or private room in a house

Proper Nouns

  • 吕用之 - Historical figure
  • 长安 - Capital city of the Tang Dynasty
  • 薛媪 - Old woman
  • 石家绿珠 - Shi Chong’s concubine Green Pearl
  • 厩 - Stable
  • 枥 - Manger
  • 堂 - Hall
  • 内室 - Inner chamber

[1060]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 众咸骇异 - Collective expression meaning 'everyone was greatly startled,' indicating a strong reaction to an unusual event.
  • 因而罢酒 - Means 'therefore, they stopped drinking,' signaling a disruption to the social gathering.
  • 好言慰之 - Means 'to comfort her with kind words,' indicating a gentle and reassuring approach.
  • 裙布椎作 - Refers to 'wearing skirts and working hard,' describing the humble life of a market woman.
  • 罗敷自有夫 - Reference to the folk tale of Luo Fu, emphasizing the protagonist's fidelity and independence.
  • 此志不可夺也 - Means 'this resolve cannot be taken away,' indicating the protagonist's firm determination.
  • 遍求禳遣 - Means 'to seek widely for exorcism or dismissal,' indicating the search for a solution to the supernatural disturbance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 罗敷 - Refers to Luo Fu, a legendary figure in Chinese folklore known for her beauty and fidelity. The reference to her story adds a layer of cultural significance to the protagonist's resolve.
  • 白马长丈余 - A supernatural element, often associated with divine or demonic forces in Chinese folklore. The size of the horse (over a zhang, a traditional Chinese unit of length) emphasizes its otherworldly nature.
  • 妖孽 - Refers to a malevolent supernatural being or force, often associated with chaos and danger in Chinese mythology.

Proper Nouns

  • 吕 - The name of the male protagonist, requiring a consistent rendering throughout the translation.
  • 女 - The name of the female protagonist, requiring a consistent rendering throughout the translation.

[1061]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有胡僧自言能禳妖 - A tricolon meaning 'there was a foreign monk who claimed he could dispel evil spirits.'
  • 此上帝玉马，为祟汝家 - Means 'this is the jade horse of the Heavenly Emperor, which has been causing calamity in your household.'
  • 非人力所能遣也 - Means 'not something that human effort can dispel.'
  • 兆不利于主人 - Means 'the omen is unfavorable for the master of the house.'
  • 将奈之何 - Means 'what can be done about it?'
  • 移之他人可代也 - Means 'it can be transferred to another person to take its place.'
  • 谁为我代耶 - Means 'who will take it for me?'
  • 良久 - Means 'for a long time' or 'after a while.'
  • 得其人矣 - Means 'I have found the person.'
  • 金帛酬僧 - Means 'to reward the monk with gold and silk.'
  • 拂衣而出 - Means 'to brush one's robe and exit,' indicating a dignified departure.
  • 薛媪至曰 - Means 'the old woman arrived and said.'
  • 故人为谁 - Means 'who is this old friend?'
  • 金部郎黄损也 - Means 'it is Huang Sun, the Lang of the Gold Department.'
  • 私喜 - Means 'secretly pleased.'
  • 所不即死者 - Means 'as long as I am not immediately killed.'
  • 意黄郎入长安，了此宿盟耳 - Means 'I intended for Huang Lang to enter Chang'an to settle this old alliance.'
  • 萧郎从此自路人矣 - Means 'Xiao Lang will henceforth be a stranger to me.'
  • 九原死骨 - A euphemism for 'the dead,' often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 奈何驱之若东西水也 - Means 'how can I be driven around like water flowing east or west?'
  • 胡僧之力也 - Means 'the power of the foreign monk.'
  • 后房奁饰 - Means 'the valuables and decorations in the inner chambers.'
  • 长须持刺投生 - Means 'the long-bearded man delivered a card to Huang Sun.'
  • 力拒不允 - Means 'strongly refused to accept.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 胡僧 - Refers to a foreign monk, likely from Central Asia or India.
  • 上帝玉马 - Refers to a jade horse of the Heavenly Emperor, a divine or supernatural entity.
  • 兆不利于主人 - Refers to an omen that is unfavorable for the master of the house.
  • 金部郎 - Refers to an official rank in the Tang Dynasty, specifically the Lang of the Gold Department.
  • 长安 - Refers to Chang'an, the capital city of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 九原 - A euphemism for the grave or the afterlife.
  • 长须 - Refers to a long-bearded man, likely a servant or messenger.
  • 刺 - Refers to a card or note, often used in formal or business contexts.

Proper Nouns

  • 胡僧 - The foreign monk.
  • 吕延 - The speaker, likely a high-ranking official or noble.
  • 薛媪 - The old woman.
  • 黄损 - The official, Huang Sun, the Lang of the Gold Department.
  • 萧郎 - The speaker's former lover or ally.

  • 崔氏女遂与轻红蹑之，同诣柳生 - '崔氏女' (Cui family daughter) is the subject, '遂' (then) introduces the action, '与轻红' (with Qinghong) is the accompaniment, '蹑之' (climb it) is the action, and '同诣柳生' (together go to Liu Sheng) is the destination.
  • 柳生惊喜 - '柳生' (Liu Sheng) is the subject, '惊喜' (surprised and delighted) is the emotional state.

  • 后本夫终寻崔氏女 - '后' (later) sets the temporal context, '本夫' (the original husband) is the subject, '终寻' (eventually found) is the verb, and '崔氏女' (Cui family daughter) is the object.
  • 知群贤里住，复兴讼夺之 - '知' (knew) is the verb, '群贤里住' (lives in Qunxian Lane) is the information, and '复兴讼夺之' (again filed a lawsuit to reclaim her) is the action.
  • 王生情深崔氏，万途求免，托以体孕，又不责而纳焉 - '王生' (Wang Sheng) is the subject, '情深崔氏' (deeply loves the Cui family) is the description, '万途求免' (tried every means to avoid it) is the action, '托以体孕' (pretended she was pregnant) is the excuse, and '又不责而纳焉' (yet did not blame her and accepted her) is the result.

  • 王生送丧，哀恸之礼至矣 - '王生' (Wang Sheng) is the subject, '送丧' (attended the funeral) is the action, and '哀恸之礼至矣' (observed the utmost mourning rites) is the description.
  • 轻红亦葬于崔氏坟侧 - '轻红' (Qinghong) is the subject, '亦葬于' (also buried in) is the action, and '崔氏坟侧' (the side of the Cui family grave) is the location.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 彼此顾盼 - They would look at each other. This is a simple but vivid description of mutual glances.
  • 女成疾数月而死 - The girl fell ill for several months and then died. '成疾' means to fall ill, and '数月' specifies the duration.

  • 偶一夕，其女忽来求合 - One night, the girl suddenly came to seek union. '偶' means 'by chance,' and '求合' implies a desire for union or intimacy.
  • 天未明去 - She left before dawn. '天未明' means 'before daybreak.'
  • 夜深复来，不知所自 - She came again late at night, but her origin was unknown. '不知所自' means 'her origin was unknown.'
  • 如此数月，遂成病，延医罔效 - This continued for several months, and she eventually fell ill; calling in doctors was ineffective. '罔效' means 'ineffective.'
  • 有司训精于医，诊其脉云 - The official who was skilled in medicine, upon diagnosing her pulse, said... '有司训' refers to an official with medical expertise.

  • 伺其再来，可坚留其随身一物为验 - Wait for her to come again and firmly keep one of her personal items as proof. '坚留' means to firmly retain, and '验' means evidence or proof.
  • 已而复来，坚留其鞋一只 - Soon after, she came again, and one of her shoes was firmly kept. '已而' means 'soon after.'
  • 司训持此鞋遍访 - The official held this shoe and went to inquire everywhere. '遍访' means to inquire everywhere.
  • 有一老妪而见堕泪云 - An old woman wept and said... '堕泪' means to weep.
  • 此亡女随身鞋也。何以入公手？ - This is the shoe of my deceased daughter. How did it come into your hands? '亡女' means deceased daughter.
  • 司训令开棺视之 - The official ordered the coffin to be opened and inspected. '开棺视之' means to open the coffin and inspect.

  • 即白之御史 - He immediately informed the censor. '白' means to inform or report.

  • 其怪遂绝 - The strange occurrences then ceased. '怪' refers to supernatural or strange occurrences.
  • 御史深德司训 - The censor deeply appreciated the official. '深德' means to deeply appreciate.
  • 及司训升教谕时，又与前御史相值 - When the official was promoted to the position of teacher, he met the former censor again. '相值' means to meet again.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 料其瘗藏丰备 - 料 means to guess or assume; 瘗藏 refers to burial goods; 丰备 means abundant and complete. This phrase suggests the woodcutter assumes the tomb contains rich burial goods.
  • 遂谋发冢 - 谋 means to plan; 发冢 means to open a tomb. This phrase indicates the woodcutter plans to rob the tomb.
  • 扶女尸起坐剥衣 - 扶 means to support; 起坐 means to sit up; 剥衣 means to strip off clothes. This describes the woodcutter supporting the girl's body, making her sit up, and stripping her clothes.
  • 我赖尔力，幸得活 - 赖 means depend on; 尔力 means your strength; 幸得活 means fortunately, I am alive. This is the girl thanking the woodcutter for saving her life.
  • 切忽害我 - 切 means certainly or especially; 忽害我 means do not harm me. This is a plea for the woodcutter not to harm her.
  • 候黄昏抱归尔家安息 - 候 means wait until; 黄昏 means dusk; 抱归 means carry back; 尔家安息 means rest in your home. This is the girl asking to be carried back to the woodcutter's home at dusk to rest.
  • 若能安好，便为尔妻 - 若能安好 means if you can take care of me well; 便为尔妻 means then you will be my husband. This is the girl offering herself as the woodcutter's wife if he takes care of her.
  • 绐樵云 - 绐 means to deceive; 樵云 means the woodcutter said. This indicates the girl deceives the woodcutter.
  • 我去南山久 - 去 means to go; 南山 means South Mountain; 久 means for a long time. This is the girl saying she has been away from South Mountain for a long time.
  • 汝办船载我一游 - 汝 means you; 办船 means arrange a boat; 载我一游 means take me on a tour. This is the girl asking the woodcutter to arrange a boat to take her on a tour.
  • 假使我家见时，喜我死而复生，必不穷问 - 假使 means if; 我家见时 means when my family sees me; 喜我死而复生 means they will be happy that I died and came back to life; 必不穷问 means they will not ask too many questions. This is the girl's reasoning for why her family will not ask too many questions if they see her.
  • 亟邀彭并膝 - 亟 means quickly; 邀 means to invite; 并膝 means to sit side by side. This is the girl quickly inviting Peng to sit beside her.
  • 道再生缘由 - 道 means to say; 再生 means reborn; 缘由 means reason. This is the girl explaining the reason for her rebirth.
  • 彭既素鄙之 - 彭 refers to Peng; 既 means already; 素 means always; 鄙之 means look down on her. This indicates Peng has always looked down on the girl.
  • 批其颊曰 - 批 means to slap; 其颊 means her cheek; 曰 means said. This describes Peng slapping the girl's cheek and speaking.
  • 死鬼，争敢白昼见形 - 死鬼 means dead ghost; 争敢 means how dare; 白昼见形 means appear in broad daylight. This is Peng's harsh words to the girl.
  • 女泣而走 - 女 means the girl; 泣 means cry; 走 means run. This describes the girl crying and running away.
  • 逐之，坠于楼下 - 逐之 means chase her; 坠于楼下 means fall from the building. This describes Peng chasing the girl, who falls from the building.
  • 视之死矣 - 视之 means look at her; 死矣 means is dead. This indicates the girl is dead after falling.
  • 樵以酒至，执彭赴里保 - 樵以酒至 means the woodcutter brought wine; 执彭赴里保 means took Peng to the village elders. This describes the woodcutter bringing wine and taking Peng to the village elders.
  • 吴氏闻而悉来 - 吴氏 refers to the Wu family; 闻 means hear; 悉来 means all came. This indicates the Wu family heard and came to the scene.
  • 守尸悲哭 - 守尸 means guard the body; 悲哭 means cry in sorrow. This describes the Wu family guarding the girl's body and crying.
  • 殊不晓所以生之故 - 殊不晓 means do not understand; 所以生之故 means the reason for her rebirth. This indicates the Wu family does not understand why the girl was reborn.
  • 并捕樵送府 - 并 means and; 捕 means arrest; 送府 means send to the government office. This describes the Wu family arresting the woodcutter and sending him to the government office.
  • 遣县尉诣墓审验 - 遣 means send; 县尉 means county magistrate; 诣 means go to; 审验 means inspect. This describes sending the county magistrate to inspect the tomb.
  • 空无一物 - 空 means empty; 无一物 means nothing. This indicates the tomb was empty.
  • 狱成，樵坐破棺见尸论死 - 狱成 means the case is closed; 樵坐 means the woodcutter is charged; 破棺见尸 means opened the coffin and saw the body; 论死 means sentenced to death. This describes the woodcutter being sentenced to death for opening the coffin and seeing the body.
  • 彭得轻比 - 彭 refers to Peng; 得 means received; 轻比 means a lighter sentence. This indicates Peng received a lighter sentence.
  • 云居寺僧了清，是时抄化到鄂 - 云居寺僧 means monk from Yunju Temple; 了清 is the monk's name; 是时 means at that time; 抄化 means begging; 到鄂 means arrived in E. This describes the monk from Yunju Temple arriving in E at that time.
  • 正睹其异 - 正睹 means just witnessed; 其异 means the strange event. This indicates the monk just witnessed the strange event.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 年十五，以棋童入侍 - The sentence introduces the boy's role and age but could be interpreted as either 'At fifteen, he served as a chess-playing servant' or 'At fifteen, he was a chess-playing servant who served.'
  • 每秋壑回朝，宴坐半闲堂，必召儿侍弈，备见宠爱 - This sentence describes a sequence of events but could be interpreted as either 'Whenever Qiuhè returned to court, he would entertain in the Half-Leisure Hall and always summon the boy to play chess, fully enjoying his favor' or 'Whenever Qiuhè returned to court, he would entertain in the Half-Leisure Hall, and the boy, who was always summoned to play chess, fully enjoyed his favor.'
  • 是时君为其家苍头，职主煎茶 - This sentence introduces the narrator's role but could be interpreted as either 'At that time, you were his family's servant in charge of making tea' or 'At that time, you were his family's servant, and your duty was to make tea.'
  • 彼此虽各有意，而内外严密，莫能得其便 - This sentence describes the mutual feelings and the social barriers but could be interpreted as either 'Although both of us had feelings for each other, the strict separation between inside and outside made it impossible to act on them' or 'Although both of us had feelings for each other, the strict separation between inside and outside prevented us from finding a way to be together.'
  • 后为同辈所觉，谗于秋壑，遂与君同赐死于西湖断桥之下 - This sentence describes the discovery and punishment but could be interpreted as either 'Later, our peers discovered us, reported to Qiuhè, and we were both executed by beheading at the Broken Bridge in West Lake' or 'Later, our peers discovered us and reported to Qiuhè, and we were both executed by beheading at the Broken Bridge in West Lake.'
  • 君今已再世为人，而儿犹在鬼录，得非命欤 - This sentence expresses the idea of reincarnation and the narrator's continued existence in the afterlife but could be interpreted as either 'You have already been reincarnated as a human, while I am still recorded in the register of the dead, is this not fate?' or 'You have already been reincarnated as a human, while I am still recorded in the register of the dead, is this not destiny?'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一睹倾城貌 - Literally means 'to see once the beauty that can topple a city,' referring to extraordinary beauty. In English, this can be rendered as 'at first sight, the beauty that could captivate a city.'
  • 尘心只自猜 - Literally means 'the worldly heart only speculates,' suggesting that the speaker's thoughts are filled with uncertainty or speculation. In English, this can be rendered as 'the worldly heart only speculates.'
  • 不随萧史去 - Literally means 'not to follow Xiao Shi away,' referring to the story of Xiao Shi, a legendary musician who flew away with a fairy. This can be rendered as 'not to follow Xiao Shi into the heavens.'
  • 拟学阿兰来 - Literally means 'to imitate Alán coming,' where Alán is likely a reference to a virtuous or idealized figure. This can be rendered as 'to imitate the virtues of Alán.'
  • 绿惨双蛾不自持 - Literally means 'the greenish sorrow of the double eyebrows cannot be controlled,' describing deep sorrow. In English, this can be rendered as 'the sorrow of the double eyebrows cannot be contained.'
  • 只缘幽恨在新诗 - Literally means 'only because of the hidden resentment in the new poem,' suggesting that the sorrow stems from a recent poem. In English, this can be rendered as 'only because of the hidden resentment in the new poem.'
  • 郎心应似琴心怨 - Literally means 'the heart of the young man should be like the resentful heart of the qin,' where the qin is a traditional Chinese zither. This can be rendered as 'the heart of the young man should be like the resentful heart of the qin.'
  • 脉脉春情更泥谁 - Literally means 'the lingering spring feelings cling to whom more,' suggesting unrequited love. In English, this can be rendered as 'the lingering spring feelings cling to whom more.'
  • 珍重佳人赠好音 - Literally means 'treasure the good news from a beautiful person,' suggesting the value of a message from someone admired. In English, this can be rendered as 'treasure the good news from a beautiful person.'
  • 彩笺方翰两情深 - Literally means 'the colorful letter and the square brush pen express deep feelings,' referring to the act of writing a letter. In English, this can be rendered as 'the colorful letter and the square brush pen express deep feelings.'
  • 薄于蝉翼难供恨 - Literally means 'as thin as a cicada's wing, it is difficult to bear the resentment,' suggesting that the medium is too delicate to convey deep emotions. In English, this can be rendered as 'as thin as a cicada's wing, it is difficult to bear the resentment.'
  • 密似蝇头未写心 - Literally means 'as dense as a fly's head, it has not yet written the heart,' suggesting that the writing is too small or too dense to fully express the heart. In English, this can be rendered as 'as dense as a fly's head, it has not yet written the heart.'
  • 疑见落 - This phrase is incomplete and ambiguous. It could mean 'doubtfully seeing it fall' or 'doubtfully seeing it drop.' In English, this can be rendered as 'doubtfully seeing it fall.'

  • 琴心 - Refers to the heart expressed through the qin (a traditional Chinese zither), often associated with unrequited love or deep emotional expression.
  • 剡溪玉叶纸 - Refers to a type of high-quality paper from Shanxi, known for its fine texture and beauty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 一睹倾城貌，尘心只自猜 - The sentence structure is straightforward, but the meaning of '尘心只自猜' requires careful interpretation to convey the sense of uncertainty or speculation.
  • 不随萧史去，拟学阿兰来 - The parallel structure suggests a contrast between following an idealized figure (萧史) and imitating a virtuous one (阿兰).
  • 绿惨双蛾不自持，只缘幽恨在新诗 - The sentence structure is clear, but the emotional depth of '绿惨双蛾不自持' needs to be preserved.
  • 郎心应似琴心怨，脉脉春情更泥谁 - The parallel structure emphasizes the unrequited love and the lingering feelings.
  • 珍重佳人赠好音，彩笺方翰两情深 - The sentence structure is clear, but the cultural significance of '彩笺方翰' needs to be preserved.
  • 薄于蝉翼难供恨，密似蝇头未写心 - The parallel structure emphasizes the difficulty in expressing deep emotions through writing.
  • 疑见落 - The incomplete phrase requires careful interpretation to convey the intended meaning.

Proper Nouns

  • 阿兰 - The name of a virtuous or idealized figure.

  • The ruler of Southern Tang, often referred to as Li Yu, known for his literary talents and tragic life.
  • The empress of Southern Tang, known for her beauty and talent.
  • A palace within the imperial complex, often associated with significant events or memorials.
  • The plum blossom, a symbol of resilience and purity in Chinese culture, often used in poetry to express deep emotions.

  • 后主 - The emperor of Southern Tang, Li Yu.

[1200]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 禄山之乱 - Refers to the An Lushan Rebellion, a major civil war during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 以诛国忠为名 - Means 'under the pretense of punishing An Guozhong,' indicating that An Lushan used this as a pretext for his rebellion.
  • 皇太子监国 - Means 'the crown prince acting as regent,' referring to the practice of having the heir apparent rule temporarily.
  • 自亲征 - Means 'to personally lead the campaign,' indicating the emperor's direct involvement in military affairs.
  • 国忠惧，泣诉妃 - Means 'An Guozhong was afraid and wept to the empress,' showing his emotional appeal to the empress.
  • 衔土请命 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to swallow earth as a plea for life,' symbolizing extreme desperation.
  • 六月潼关失守 - Refers to the fall of Tongguan Pass in June, a significant military defeat.
  • 上幸蜀 - Means 'the emperor fled to Shu,' indicating the emperor's escape to the Sichuan region.
  • 至马嵬驿，兵乱，杀国忠，围未解 - Describes the events at Mawei Post, where the army rebelled, killed An Guozhong, and initially did not disband.
  • 上出问其故，高力士以贵妃为言 - Means 'the emperor came out to ask the reason, and Gao Lishi mentioned the concubine,' indicating the role of Gao Lishi in the decision.
  • 驿有小巷，上不忍回行宫，于巷中倚杖欹首而立 - Describes the emperor's reluctance to return to the imperial palace and his emotional state.
  • 京兆司韦锷谏曰 - Means 'Wei E, the magistrate of Jingzhao, advised,' indicating the role of Wei E in the events.
  • 愿陛下割恩，以宁国家 - Means 'I hope Your Majesty will sever this affection to stabilize the country,' a direct plea to the emperor.
  • 逡巡入行宫，使力士赐妃死 - Means 'after hesitating, the emperor entered the imperial palace and ordered Gao Lishi to have the concubine executed,' detailing the execution order.
  • 妃泣涕呜咽，语不胜情 - Describes the concubine's emotional state before her execution.
  • 大家好住，妾诚负国，死不恨矣 - Means 'May Your Majesty live well, I truly betrayed the country, and I have no regrets in dying,' a final plea from the concubine.
  • 乞容礼佛 - Means 'I request permission to pay respects to the Buddha,' a final wish of the concubine.
  • 力士遂缢之于佛堂前之梨树下 - Describes the method and location of the execution.
  • 才绝，而南海进荔枝至 - Means 'just as she died, fresh lychees arrived from the South Seas,' a poignant detail.
  • 上观之，长号数四，使力士祭之 - Describes the emperor's emotional reaction and the subsequent ritual.
  • 以绣衾覆体，置于驿亭中 - Means 'the emperor covered her body with a brocade quilt and placed it in the posthouse,' detailing the post-execution arrangements.
  • 六军乃解围 - Means 'the six armies then dispersed,' indicating the resolution of the crisis.
  • 瘗于西郭之外一里许道北坎下 - Describes the burial site.
  • 妃时年三十八 - Indicates the age of the concubine at the time of her death.
  • 此去剑门，鸟啼花落，水绿山青，无非助朕悲悼妃子之情耳 - Means 'From here to Jianmen Pass, the birds sing, flowers fall, the water is green, and the mountains are blue, all of which help to deepen my sorrow for the concubine,' a poetic reflection on the journey.
  • 上至斜谷口，属霖雨涉旬 - Means 'The emperor reached Xiegu Pass, where it rained continuously for ten days,' describing the weather conditions.
  • 于栈道雨中，闻铃声隔山相应 - Means 'in the rain on the mountain road, he heard the sound of bells echoing across the mountains,' a sensory detail.
  • 因采其声为《雨霖铃》曲，以寄恨焉 - Means 'he composed the *Yulinling* melody based on the sound, to express his resentment,' a musical tribute to his loss.

  • 赖诸名妓醵钱而葬 - A phrase meaning 'relying on the money raised by the courtesans to bury him.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 阿母桃花方似锦，王孙草色正如烟 - Poetic description using '阿母' (A mother) and '王孙' (prince or noble) metaphorically. '桃花方似锦' (peach blossoms are like brocade) and '草色正如烟' (grass color is like smoke) vividly describe natural beauty.
  • 不须更向沧溟望，惆怅欢娱恰一年 - Uses '沧溟' (vast ocean) metaphorically to suggest no need to seek further. '惆怅欢娱恰一年' (sorrow and joy have been exactly one year) indicates a period of mixed emotions.
  • 东美何故频见梦中乎 - Rhetorical question asking why '东美' (Dongmei) frequently appears in dreams.
  • 素服拜奠，抚柩哀号，一恸而绝 - '素服拜奠' (paying respects in plain clothes), '抚柩哀号' (embracing the coffin and wailing), and '一恸而绝' (dying from a single great grief) describe actions and emotions associated with mourning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 薛到外国，未行册礼，旌节晓夕有声，旋染疾 - '薛' (Xue) is a person's name, '外国' (foreign country) refers to a place outside the current domain. '册礼' (imperial investiture ceremony) is a formal ritual for bestowing titles or honors. '旌节' (banner and staff) are symbols of authority and rank.
  • 苗摄大使行礼 - '苗' (Miao) is another person's name, '摄大使' (acting as the ambassador) indicates that he temporarily took on the role of an official.
  • 薛旅榇回及青州，东美乃请告至驿，素服拜奠，抚柩哀号，一恸而绝 - '青州' (Qingzhou) is a place name, '请告至驿' (requesting leave to go to the post station) shows the act of traveling to pay respects.
  • 薄少君，娄东秀士沈承妻也 - '薄少君' (Bo Shaojun) is a person's name, '娄东秀士沈承' (Loudong scholar Shen Cheng) refers to a man named Shen Cheng, who was a scholar from Loudong.
  • 薄为诗百首悼之 - '薄' (Bo) is the name of the woman, '诗百首' (hundred poems) indicates the extent of her mourning poetry.
  • 及期，少君亦逝 - '及期' (by the time) indicates that the event happened at a specific point, '少君亦逝' (Shaojun also passed away) shows that she died around the same time.

  • 今日我悲君不哭，先离烦恼是便宜 - Suggests that today, I mourn for you, but you do not cry; it is advantageous to leave behind troubles first.
  • 饥肠寒骨儒非易，饰面违心在更难 - Indicates that being a scholar is not easy when one is hungry and cold, and it is even more difficult to maintain a facade while going against one's true feelings.
  • 地上有身无放处，不知地下可相安 - Expresses the idea that there is no place to rest in the world, and it is unknown whether peace can be found in the afterlife.

  • 会今当更生 - Means 'will be reborn now.'
  • 心相爱乐，故来相就 - Means 'because we love each other, I have come to be with you.'
  • 如此五六夕 - Means 'for several consecutive nights.'
  • 忽然昼见 - Means 'suddenly appeared during the day.'
  • 衣服薰香殊绝 - Means 'wearing clothes with a special fragrance.'
  • 遂为夫妇，寝息 - Means 'became husband and wife, sleeping together.'
  • 衣皆有袴，如处女 - Means 'wearing clothes with skirts, like a maiden.'
  • 取之，啼泣呼言发冢 - Means 'took it, crying and shouting that the tomb had been disturbed.'
  • 具陈本末 - Means 'explained the whole story.'

  • Other phrases like '愿偿平生之志耳' express a personal ambition and must be understood in the context of the protagonist's goals, while '男女之际，大欲存焉' describes a natural human desire and must be read in the context of the relationship between the protagonists. '情苟相得，虽父母不能制也' emphasizes the strength of mutual feelings and must be read in the context of the relationship dynamics. '愿以身为厮养' expresses a willingness to serve and must be understood in the context of the protagonist's humility. Idiomatic Expressions

  • 漂母而下 - Refers to a virtuous woman (漂母) and her descendants, emphasizing the continuity of virtue.
  • 數百年又得散樂女 - Means 'after several centuries, another virtuous woman (散樂女) appears,' highlighting the recurrence of such figures.
  • 擁素封而坐視人饥寒者 - Refers to wealthy, idle men who sit by and watch others suffer from hunger and cold, criticizing their inaction.
  • 視兹妇能不愧死 - Means 'to see this woman, how could they not feel ashamed to die?' expressing the moral superiority of the woman over the men.
  • 劉道真少時，嘗漁草澤 - Describes Liu Daozhen's youth, fishing in the marshes, indicating his humble origins.
  • 而老妪聞其歌啸，知非常人 - Means 'an old woman heard his singing and knew he was not an ordinary person,' suggesting his exceptional character.
  • 殺豚進之 - Means 'she killed a pig to offer him,' showing her generosity.
  • 道真食盡，了不謝 - Means 'Liu Daozhen ate it all without thanking her,' highlighting his rudeness.
  • 又進一豚，食半而去 - Means 'she offered him another pig, and he ate half before leaving,' further illustrating his behavior.
  • 後為吏部郎，妪兒時為令史，乃超用之 - Means 'later, Liu Daozhen became a Minister of Personnel, and the old woman's son, who was a minor official, was promoted,' showing the consequences of the old woman's kindness.
  • 此漂母之報也 - Means 'this is the reward of the virtuous woman,' linking the events to the earlier reference to 漂母.
  • 歐陽彬困於淮南，歌人瑞卿，以家財資之入蜀 - Means 'Ouyang Bin was in distress in Huainan, and the singer Ruqing used his family wealth to support him in entering Sichuan,' showing another act of kindness.
  • 及貴，卒偕老 - Means 'when he became wealthy, they lived together until old age,' indicating the long-term benefits of the kindness.
  • 此散樂女之報也 - Means 'this is the reward of the virtuous woman (散樂女),' linking the events to the earlier reference to 散樂女.
  • 雖然，彼皆女中丈夫，非望報者也 - Means 'although, they were all women of great virtue, not seeking reward,' emphasizing their selflessness.
  • 夫漂母與散樂女之不朽千秋也，豈在贈金乞娶時哉 - Means 'the immortality of the virtuous women and the virtuous woman (散樂女) for thousands of years, is not in the giving of gold or asking for marriage, but in their actions,' highlighting the true nature of their virtue.

  • 妻美而艳 - Describes the wife as beautiful and attractive. '美' means beautiful, and '艳' means gorgeous or seductive.
  • 夫妇之爱甚笃 - Indicates a deep and sincere love between the couple. '笃' means sincere or profound.
  • 某商于粤，久不归 - The merchant has been trading in Guangdong (Yue) for a long time and hasn't returned home. '商于粤' means 'trading in Yue,' and '久不归' means 'hasn't returned for a long time.'
  • 其家近市楼居 - The merchant's home is near the market and is a two-story building. '近市' means 'near the market,' and '楼居' means 'two-story house.'
  • 妇偶当窗垂帘外望 - The wife happens to be by the window, looking out through the curtain. '偶' means 'happens to,' '当窗' means 'by the window,' and '垂帘外望' means 'looking out through the curtain.'
  • 忽见美男子，貌类其夫 - She suddenly sees a handsome man who looks like her husband. '忽见' means 'suddenly sees,' '貌类其夫' means 'looks like her husband.'
  • 乃启帘流盼，既觉其误，赧然而避 - She then opens the curtain to look again, realizes her mistake, and blushes as she turns away. '启帘' means 'opens the curtain,' '流盼' means 'looks around,' '既觉其误' means 'realizes her mistake,' and '赧然而避' means 'blushes and turns away.'
  • 男子新安人，客二年矣 - The man is from Xian'an (a place in ancient China) and has been a guest (trading) there for two years. '新安人' means 'from Xian'an,' and '客二年矣' means 'has been a guest for two years.'
  • 见楼上美人盼己，深以为念 - He sees the beautiful woman on the upper floor looking at him and deeply remembers her. '见楼上美人盼己' means 'sees the beautiful woman on the upper floor looking at him,' and '深以为念' means 'deeply remembers her.'
  • 叩姓名于市东鬻珠老媪 - He asks the elderly woman selling pearls in the eastern market for her name. '叩姓名' means 'asks for the name,' '市东' means 'eastern market,' and '鬻珠老媪' means 'elderly woman selling pearls.'
  • 因遗重贿，求计通之 - He then gives her a large bribe to find a way to communicate with the woman. '遗重贿' means 'gives a large bribe,' and '求计通之' means 'finds a way to communicate with her.'
  • 新安客哀祈不已 - The Xian'an guest continues to beg pitifully. '哀祈不已' means 'continues to beg pitifully.'

[1259]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 新安人数问媪期，辄曰：“未未。” - 新安人 refers to a person from the region of Xin'an (modern-day Anhui Province). 辄曰：“未未。” is a colloquial expression meaning 'he always says 'not yet, not yet.' The repetition of '未' emphasizes uncertainty or delay.
  • 及至秋月，过谓媪曰：“初谋柳下，条叶未黄，约及垂阴，子已成实。过此渐秃，行将白雪侵枝矣！ - 及至秋月 means 'by the time autumn arrived.' 初谋柳下 refers to an initial plan or arrangement under a willow tree. 条叶未黄 means 'the branches and leaves were not yet yellow,' symbolizing the early stage of something. 约及垂阴 means 'until the shade is fully cast,' implying a period of growth or development. 子已成实 means 'the fruit has already ripened,' symbolizing the completion of a plan. 过此渐秃，行将白雪侵枝矣 means 'after this, it will gradually become bare, and soon snow will cover the branches,' symbolizing decline or the end of a cycle.
  • 媪曰：“今夕随老身入，须着精神，成败系此。不然，虚废半年也。” - 今夕随老身入 means 'this evening, follow me inside.' 须着精神 means 'you must be alert and focused.' 成败系此 means 'the success or failure depends on this,' emphasizing the importance of the moment. 不然，虚废半年也 means 'otherwise, half a year will be wasted in vain,' highlighting the urgency of the situation.
  • 因授之计。 - 因授之计 means 'then she gave him a plan,' indicating that the woman provided a strategy or method.
  • 媪每夜黑至妇家，是夕，阴与新安人同入，而伏之寝门之外。 - 每夜黑至妇家 means 'every night she would go to the woman's house in the dark.' 阴与新安人同入 means 'secretly with the Xin'an man, she entered together.' 而伏之寝门之外 means 'and she hid outside the bedroom door,' indicating a strategic position.
  • 媪与妇酌于房，两声甚戚，笑剧加殷。 - 酌于房 means 'they drank in the room,' indicating a social or intimate setting. 两声甚戚 means 'their voices were very sad,' suggesting a melancholic or serious tone. 笑剧加殷 means 'they laughed and joked more earnestly,' indicating a shift in mood.
  • 媪强侍儿酒，侍儿不胜，醉卧他所。 - 强侍儿酒 means 'she forced the maid to drink,' indicating coercion or insistence. 不胜，醉卧他所 means 'the maid couldn't handle it and fell asleep elsewhere,' suggesting the maid's inability to cope.
  • 独两人闭门深饮，各已微酣。 - 独两人闭门深饮 means 'the two of them closed the door and drank deeply,' indicating privacy and indulgence. 各已微酣 means 'they were both slightly tipsy,' suggesting a state of intoxication.
  • 适有飞蛾来火上，媪佯以扇扑之，灯灭，伪启门点灯，复佯笑曰：“忘携烛去。” - 适有飞蛾来火上 means 'just then, a moth flew to the flame,' indicating a sudden event. 媪佯以扇扑之 means 'the woman pretended to swat it with a fan,' suggesting a feigned action. 灯灭，伪启门点灯 means 'the lamp went out, and she pretended to open the door to relight it,' indicating a ruse. 复佯笑曰：“忘携烛去。” means 'she pretended to laugh and said, 'I forgot to bring a candle,' suggesting a cover-up.
  • 折旋之际，则已暗导其人于卧榻矣。 - 折旋之际 means 'at the turn,' indicating a moment of opportunity. 则已暗导其人于卧榻矣 means 'she had already secretly led the man to the bed,' suggesting a hidden maneuver.
  • 顷之，辞以夜深火尽，复闭门。 - 顷之 means 'after a while,' indicating a short period of time. 辞以夜深火尽 means 'she excused herself by saying it was too late and the fire had gone out,' suggesting a reason for leaving.
  • 妇畏暗，数数呼媪。 - 妇畏暗 means 'the woman was afraid of the dark,' indicating a fear or discomfort. 数数呼媪 means 'she repeatedly called for the woman,' suggesting a need for reassurance.
  • 媪曰：“老身当同帷作伴耳。” - 老身当同帷作伴耳 means 'I will stay with you in the same tent,' indicating a promise of companionship.
  • 乃挟其人登妇床，妇犹以为媪也，启被抚其身，曰：“姥体滑如是！” - 乃挟其人登妇床 means 'she then took the man and led him to the woman's bed,' indicating a physical action. 妇犹以为媪也 means 'the woman still thought it was the woman,' suggesting a mistaken identity. 启被抚其身，曰：“姥体滑如是！” means 'she pulled back the covers and touched his body, saying, 'Your body is so smooth!'' indicating a physical interaction.
  • 其人不言，腾身而上，妇已神狂，听其轻薄而已。 - 其人不言，腾身而上 means 'the man said nothing and climbed up,' indicating a passive or submissive action. 妇已神狂，听其轻薄而已 means 'the woman was already in a trance and let him do as he pleased,' suggesting a state of euphoria or surrender.
  • 欢毕，始问为何人。媪乃前谢罪，述新安客爱慕之意。 - 欢毕 means 'after they were done,' indicating the end of an activity. 始问为何人 means 'she then asked who he was,' suggesting curiosity or confusion. 媪乃前谢罪，述新安客爱慕之意 means 'the woman then apologized and explained the Xin'an man's feelings of admiration,' indicating an explanation or justification.
  • 妇业堕术中，遂不能舍，相爱逾于夫妇。 - 妇业堕术中 means 'the woman had already fallen into the trap,' indicating a loss of control or agency. 遂不能舍 means 'she could no longer let go,' suggesting a state of addiction or obsession. 相爱逾于夫妇 means 'they loved each other more than a husband and wife,' indicating a deep emotional connection.
  • 将一年，新安人赠费已及千金。 - 将一年 means 'for a year,' indicating a period of time. 新安人赠费已及千金 means 'the Xin'an man had already given her a thousand gold pieces,' indicating a significant financial transaction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 新安人 - Refers to a person from the region of Xin'an (modern-day Anhui Province), which was known for its silk production and trade during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 媪 - Refers to an elderly woman, often used in classical Chinese to denote a wise or experienced woman.
  • 妇 - Refers to a married woman, often used in classical Chinese to denote a woman in a domestic setting.
  • 千金 - Refers to a large sum of money, often used metaphorically to denote a woman of high value or status.

  • 及至秋月，过谓媪曰：“初谋柳下，条叶未黄，约及垂阴，子已成实。过此渐秃，行将白雪侵枝矣！ - This long sentence is a metaphorical description of a plan or arrangement, with the willow tree symbolizing growth and decline.
  • 媪曰：“今夕随老身入，须着精神，成败系此。不然，虚废半年也。” - This sentence is a conditional statement.
